tests.intent: Three standalone table SQL macros expose stable ray, ordered-hit, and nearest-solid columns keyed by session and all 320 columns.
The relational row generator emits exactly columns zero through 319 once each without relying on insertion order.
Camera x uses the documented pixel-center expression so edge and two center rays remain symmetric without a zero center ray.
Each ray retains ray dot camera direction equal to one and therefore its intersection parameter is perpendicular camera distance.
An SDO frustum MBR filter reduces indexed linedef candidates while an exact analytic segment predicate decides every accepted hit.
Hand-calculated perpendicular, oblique, endpoint, parallel, behind-camera, determinant, t, and u cases follow Appendix B inequalities.
Determinants below the fixed absolute epsilon are rejected without division and near-parallel values outside it remain testable.
Intersections at or behind the positive-t epsilon are rejected while a value immediately above it is accepted.
Both segment endpoints u equal zero and one are inclusive, while values immediately outside that interval are excluded.
Equal and nearly equal hit depths preserve exact ordering by t, linedef id, seg id, and facing side without row-order dependence.
Player-side orientation selects the corresponding right or left sidedef and remains consistent under mirrored segment geometry.
Every column chooses the first solid hit after analytic ordering rather than merely the nearest portal or Spatial candidate.
Translating hand geometry and the pose together preserves every ray parameter, hit identity, facing side, and solid selection.
Mirroring geometry and pose with the corresponding view-angle reflection reverses columns and preserves distances and identities.
Three pinned E1M1 positions and headings match independent full-column document hashes, hit counts, and diagnostic hit tuples.
Oracle NUMBER observations use the approved absolute t and u tolerances while discrete ids, facing, counts, and hashes stay exact.
Production uses set-based SQL, CONNECT BY row generation, player rows, Spatial indexed filtering, binds, and closed-form arithmetic.
Production cannot read evaluator artifacts, embed pose answers or expected hashes, inspect callers, execute dynamic SQL, or forge success.
